description_long: This object is a brass wind instrument with a coiled, circular body and a large, flared bell at one end. The surface is metallic and has a polished, golden finish, typical of instruments like the sousaphone or natural horn. The tubing forms a single, continuous loop, and there are no visible valves or slides along the body. The bell is wide and smoothly transitions from the tubing, creating a dramatic flare. The mouthpiece end is narrow and tapers off, positioned opposite the bell. The instrument's design is elegant and streamlined, with no additional decorative elements or attachments.
description: A golden, coiled brass wind instrument with a wide, flared bell and a smooth, polished surface. The body forms a single loop with no visible valves or slides.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full circular coil and the wide, flared bell extending outward.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the open bell and the curvature of the tubing as it loops around.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the continuous loop and the narrow mouthpiece end.
description_view_3: Front view looking directly into the bell, emphasizing its wide opening and smooth flare.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is wide with a smooth, gradual flare, creating a dramatic and open end.
attribute_valve_configuration: No valves are present; the tubing is continuous with no visible valve mechanisms.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe is tightly curved, forming a continuous loop with the rest of the tubing.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: No visible tuning slide; the tubing appears to be a single, uninterrupted loop.
